title: @placeholder
description: The @placeholder directive displays custom content while lazy or deferred components and islands are loading.

sections.title: #Basic usage with lazy components
sections.content:
For single-file and multi-file components, use @placeholder to specify what displays during loading:
When rendered with <livewire:revenue lazy />, the placeholder displays until the component loads.
The @placeholder directive works for single-file and multi-file components. For class-based components, use the placeholder() method instead.
The placeholder and component must share the same root element type. If your placeholder uses <div>, your component must also use <div>.

examples.code:
amount = Transaction::monthToDate()->sum('amount'); }};?> @placeholder
@endplaceholder 
Revenue this month: {{ $amount }}

sections.title: #Usage with islands
sections.content:
Use @placeholder inside lazy islands to customize loading states:
The placeholder appears while the island is loading, then gets replaced with the actual content.

examples.code:
@island(lazy: true) @placeholder
@endplaceholder 
Revenue: {{ $this->revenue }} 
@endisland

sections.title: #Skeleton placeholders
sections.content: Placeholders are ideal for skeleton loaders that match your content's layout:

examples.code:
@placeholder
@endplaceholder 

{{ $post->title }}

{{ $post->content }}

sections.title: #Learn more
sections.content:
For lazy component loading, see the Lazy Loading documentation.
For island loading states, see the Islands documentation.
